notes: Re-approved by Reg 2018/1981 (13 Dec 2018), in force from Jan 2019. Capped at 28 kg Cu/ha per 7 years; classified as candidate for substitution. Renewal review pending in 2025.
Same Reg 2018/1981; same 28 kg/ha 7-year cap; widely used in HU.
Same Reg 2018/1981; same 28 kg/ha 7-year cap.

Originally approved 1 Jan 2010 by Commission Directive 2009/70/EC (incl. in Annex I to Dir 91/414/EEC). Approval period repeatedly extended via Reg 2017/555, Reg 2020/1511 (to 31 Dec 2021), and Reg 2023/2592. Current expiry not verified from a single primary source; renewal under Reg 844/2012 procedure ongoing. Backbone of powdery mildew control in Hungary.

Non-renewal Reg 2020/2087 (14 Dec 2020). Approval expired 4 January 2021. MS authorisations withdrawn by 4 July 2021. Grace period for use of stocks expired 4 January 2022. Loss is significant for HU growers.

Approved 1 June 2011 by Commission Implementing Directive 2011/41/EU. Approval period extended via Reg 2018/1266, Reg 2020/2007 and most recently Reg 2024/1206 (29 Apr 2024) — extension to 31 July 2029 reported by national MS registers (e.g. UK HSE). Common contact partner for downy mildew programmes.
Approved 1 August 2013 by Reg 188/2013. Modern systemic against Plasmopara.

RENEWED by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2390. New approval applies from 1 November 2024 until 31 October 2039 — a full 15-year renewal. Original approval was 1 February 2007 (Commission Directive 2006/74/EC). Specialised powdery mildew product. Latvia/Slovakia were rapporteur/co-rapporteur for renewal.

Approved 1 August 2008 by Commission Directive 2008/44/EC (incl. in Annex I of Dir 91/414/EEC). Approval period extended several times (most recently Reg 2022/708 of 5 May 2022 and Reg 2023/918 of 4 May 2023). Renewal application submitted June 2015 but delayed pending endocrine-disruptor assessment under Reg 844/2012. EU General Court (T-94/23, 19 Nov 2025) annulled certain extension regulations. Often co-formulated with kresoxim-methyl or pyraclostrobin.
Approved 1 February 2014 by Reg 802/2013. Modern SDHI used in vineyards.

Approved 1 January 2013 by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 589/2012 of 4 July 2012. Approval period extended by Reg 2020/2007 to 31 May 2025, then by Reg 2025/787 (24 April 2025) by a further 29 months (renewal assessment by RMS not finalised). Resulting expiry approximately 31 October 2027. SDHI fungicide; resistance management important.

Approved 1 September 2009 by Commission Directive 2008/125/EC (entered into force on that date). Conditions of approval amended by Reg 921/2014. Approval period extended several times, most recently by Reg 2023/1446 (12 July 2023) to allow time for the endocrine-disruptor assessment under Reg 844/2012. Older DMI; under endocrine-disruptor scrutiny.

Approved 1 January 2009 by Commission Directive 2008/69/EC. Conditions of approval amended by Reg 1100/2011. Approval period extended successively by Reg 2019/1589, Reg 2020/1511, Reg 2021/1449, Reg 2022/1480 and most recently Reg 2023/2592 (21 November 2023). Widely used DMI.

Approved 1 April 2010 by Commission Directive 2009/154/EC. Approval period extended by Reg 2017/1527 (to 31 March 2023), then by Reg 2023/114 (to 31 March 2024), and most recently by Reg 2024/324 of 19 January 2024. Specialised powdery mildew product.

Originally approved 1 May 2014 by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 1177/2013 of 20 November 2013. Approval expired 30 April 2024 because no applicant submitted a renewal application (Bayer withdrew due to endocrine-disruptor data requirements). Loss for HU mealybug/leafhopper vector control. Sale and inventory grace period to ~April 2025.
Initially approved 2001, re-approved 2016. Approval period extended via Reg 2023/114. Pyrethroid used in FD vector control campaigns.

Approved 1 November 2003 by Commission Directive 2003/5/EC (10 January 2003). Conditions of approval extended via Reg 2018/1262, Reg 2020/1511 and most recently Reg 2023/1757 (11 September 2023). Authorised for Scaphoideus titanus control in several EU MS.

First approved by Directive 2004/99/EC (effective 2005). Renewed by Reg 2018/113 (24 January 2018) until 28 February 2033. Only neonicotinoid still allowed outdoors in EU; KEY tool for leafhopper vector control in FD strategy.
Outdoor use banned 2018 (Reg 2018/785). Loss significant for FD vector control.
Outdoor use banned 2018 (Reg 2018/783).
Outdoor use banned 2018 (Reg 2018/784).
Non-renewal Commission decision 13 January 2020; withdrawn 3 February 2020; approval expired 30 April 2020. EFSA cited groundwater and reproductive toxicity concerns.
Non-renewal Reg 2019/1090 (26 June 2019); previously used against Scaphoideus titanus.
Non-renewal Reg 2020/18 (10 January 2020). MS withdrew authorisations by 16 February 2020; grace period expired 16 April 2020.
First EU approval 1 July 2002. First renewal 12 December 2017 (5 years to 15 Dec 2022, extended one year). Second renewal 2023 for 10 years until 15 December 2033 (Reg 2023/2660).

RENEWED by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/351 of 18 February 2026; new approval applies from 1 April 2026. Originally approved 1 February 2007 by Commission Directive 2007/6/EC. Approval period had been extended by Reg 2023/689 and Reg 2025/99 pending the renewal decision. Approved for organic viticulture.

Multiple Bt strains used in viticulture, all approved under Reg 1107/2009. Bt subsp. kurstaki strains ABTS-351, PB 54, SA 11, SA 12, EG 2348 RENEWED by Commission Implementing Regulations (EU) 2023/998, 2023/999, 2023/1000 etc. (23 May 2023). Extension Reg 2023/689 (20 March 2023) covered the transition. Standard organic moth control for Lobesia botrana.

Listed as aluminium silicate (kaolin / kaolin calcined) in EU Reg 540/2011. EFSA peer review of renewal published 2022 (EFSA Journal 7637); rapporteur MS Greece, co-rapporteur France. Renewal Implementing Regulation has not yet been identified from a single primary source; original Annex inclusion date and current expiry unconfirmed. Particle film barrier; used in organic systems against sunburn and leafhoppers.
